name: tidy
description: Tidy-as-you-touch: after each edit it formats and lint-checks just the file you touched (fixing only what's safe), surfaces its blast radius (the dependents to check), and flags it for decomposition past a size budget — keeping an actively-worked project trending down in complexity. Findings-only edit-time linters run the project's own tools for Go (goimports/gofumpt + golangci-lint), web (eslint incl. jsx-a11y, stylelint), Python (ruff) and shell (shellcheck). A pre-write hook blocks hardcoded secrets before they reach disk. You run your own tests — tidy does NOT run them. Its Stop hook makes one non-blocking post-work pass on a dirty tree: import cycles touching your change, and a debt-prune nudge when files cross the size budget (weight report via tidy-distill, cuts routed through the task-queue review loop). Two opt-in, off-by-default test-existence gates are available for those who want them (coverage ratchet, regression gate on scar-tissue files) — all detect-and-run, installs/invents nothing. Goes quiet (one-line re-anchor) once recorded in the project's CLAUDE.md. The same whole-project weight report is also available on demand via /tidy:audit, which auto-queues every finding as a cleanup task.
